name: Deferred - Debt - 2
description: Second returns for old debts from earlier chapters of a life.

results.description: [money -1d6*20] The debt from {origin} was finally collected in coin you would have used better yourself.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money -60] The old obligation from {origin} was settled only after it had become more expensive than the original need.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [contact] A debt from {origin} was discharged in service instead of silver, leaving behind an uneasy connection.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money -30] The claim from {origin} was met, but only after it stripped comfort from your purse for longer than you expected.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [social -1] The debt from {origin} was no secret by the time it ended, and that cost its own coin.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money 1d6*20] An old account from {origin} unexpectedly turned in your favor when the other side proved too greedy to be believed.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [contact] The debt from {origin} ended in an arrangement that was not trust, but could imitate it when needed.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money -40] You paid what {origin} had left owing, and found relief only slightly sweeter than resentment.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [social -1] A debt from {origin} was settled in public enough that the shame survived the payment.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money -20] The old collector from {origin} took less than feared and left you suspicious of mercy thereafter.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [contact] What began as debt in {origin} ended as a hard, useful acquaintance who still remembered the balance.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money 1d6*10] The claim from {origin} collapsed under challenge, leaving you with a little coin and more satisfaction than profit.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money -50] The debt from {origin} did not ruin you, but it taught you how long old need can live.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [social 1] The debt born in {origin} was finally discharged so cleanly that others could only call it honorable.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [money 20] An old debt from {origin} was repaid to you instead, though too late to count as justice.
results.name: debt-2

results.description: [contact] The long account from {origin} ended in mutual caution and a respect too narrow to be friendship.
results.name: debt-2
